LAW AND MOTION, 302, RESPONDENT STAN SCHULDINER'S MOTION TO USE PSEUDONYM IS DENIED. STAN SCHULDINER HAS USED HIS REAL NAME IN PLEADINGS THROUGHOUT THIS 45-MONTH LITIGATION, WHICH IS NOW ON APPEAL. HIS MOTION TO NOW USE A PSEUDONYM IS DENIED. I AGREE WITH THE ORDER DENYING SCHULDINER'S PRIOR MOTION FOR THE SAME RELIEF. AS JUDGE QUINN FOUND, USE OF A PSEUDONYM IMPAIRS IMPORTANT PUBLIC RIGHTS TO ACCESS COURT RECORDS (SEE, E.G., CRC 2.550(C)) AND VIOLATES THE REQUIREMENT THAT A COMPLAINT INCLUDE "NAMES OF ALL THE PARTIES" (CCP 422.40). MOREOVER, SCHULDINER'S NEW MOTION AGAIN FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THE KIND OF PREJUDICE THAT COULD OUTWEIGH PUBLIC RIGHTS AND STATUTORY REQUIREMENTS. (SEE DOE V. LINCOLN UNIFIED SCHOOL DIST. (2010) 188 CAL.APP.4TH 758, 767.) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion For New Trial; Motion For Reconsideration; Motion To Vacate Judgment; Motion For Relief From Judgment; Motion To Amend Judgment; Memo
Off Calendar Jun-18-2020 Continued To Jun-22-20 At 09:31 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. MATHEW M. WRENSHALL, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP (Tel: 213-457-8076) appearing via CourtCall for petitioners Incomm Financial Services, Inc. and the Bankcorp Bank;
Respondent STAN SCHULDNER (Tel: 415-724-6862) appearing via CourtCall in pro per.

HEARING RE: RESPONDENT STAN SCHULDNER'S Motion For New Trial; Motion For Reconsideration; Motion To Vacate Judgment; Motion For Relief From Judgment; Motion To Amend Judgment.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear.

The Court strikes Mr. Schuldner's statement of disqualification per CCP 170.3 because that statement fails to disclose any alleged ground for disqualification. As a matter of law, the mere mention of an appeal of a trial judge's ruling in an order issued by that trial judge is not grounds for disqualification.

The Court denies Mr. Schuldner's peremptory challenge per CCP 170.6 because the challenge is untimely since Judge Kahn has previously made determinations of contested facts in this case and a 170.6 challenge may not be made to prevent a judge from ruling on a motion for reconsideration.

As to Mr. Schuldner's motion for new trial/motion for reconsideration, the Court raises its concern that it currently has no jurisdiction over the matter due the pending appeal filed by Mr. Schuldner on April 6, 2020.

The Court grants Mr. Schuldner's request to postpone today's hearing so that he may file a withdrawal of that appeal. The Court continues the matter to June 22, 2020 in Dept. 302 tobe called at 9:00 a.m. The Court indicates that if by June 22, 2020 there is not withdrawal of the appeal or an order that the appeal is no longer, the matter will have to be postponed again.

The Court grants Mr. Schuldner's request to submit supplemental briefing on the issue of the appeal. The Court instructs Mr. Schuldner to file and serve his brief by today and to provide a courtesy copy to the Court by email sent to Department505@sftc.org or by hard copy as soon as possible.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Motion To Amend Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mathew Wrenshall, esq. (213-457-8076) of Reed Smith LLP for Petitioner.
Stan Schuldiner (718-761-0059) Respondent in propria persona.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Motion To Amend Judgment:

Respondent and cross-complainant Stan Schuldiner's motion to amend judgment is denied. Assuming without deciding that the motion is timely and the court has the authority to grant the motion at this stage of the case, the motion is in substance a request to reconsider the October 10, 2019 order yet Mr. Schuldiner offers no reason why the grounds for the motion were not or could not have been raised prior to issuance of the October 10, 2019 order or any other reason why the court should revisit that order. The important policy of finality of litigation, particularly in a case that has been extensively litigated like this one, requires that a request to revisit a post-judgment costs order that was itself heavily litigated be supported by good cause to revisit the costs order. This is especially true when the motion is made after an appeal of the underlying judgment was dismissed. Because no new facts, law or circumstances or any other good cause has been shown to support revisiting the October 10, 2019 order, the motion is denied.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion To Use Pseudonym; Memo ** Redacted **
Off Calendar Apr-05-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mathew M. Wrenshall, esq., Reed Smith llp 213-457-0876, appearing for Plaintiff (CourtCall)

No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT STAN SCHULDINER'S MOTION To Use Pseudonym

DENIED. Stan Schuldiner has used his real name in pleadings throughout this 45-month litigation, which is now on appeal. His motion to now use a pseudonym is DENIED. I agree with the order denying Schuldiner's prior motion for the same relief. As Judge Quinn found, use of a pseudonym impairs important public rights to access court records (see, e.g., CRC 2.550(c)) and violates the requirement that a complaint include "names of all the parties" (CCP 422.40). Moreover, Schuldiner's new motion again fails to demonstrate the kind of prejudice that could outweigh public rights and statutory requirements. (See Doe v. Lincoln Unified School Dist. (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 758, 767.)

order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Mtn To File A 4th Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stan Schuldiner, T:714.761.0059, defendant/cross complainant and moving party, appearing on his own behalf;
Mathew Wrenshall, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP, T:213.457.8076, for plaintiff/cross defendant and opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STAN SCHULDINER'S Motion To Amend is GRANTED. Mr. Schuldiner must label his new pleading a "fifth amended cross-complaint" and he has leave to file it no later than January 12, 2018. Mr. Schuldiner has shown good cause for his additional factual allegations based on his recent receipt of discovery supporting these allegations. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings at any time, particularly where, as here, there is no legal prejudice to cross-defendants by doing so. The court's previous comments about a last opportunity to amend are fairly construed to cover only matters that Mr. Schuldiner then knew or should have known, not matters which had not yet been disclosed to him in discovery.

Mr. Schuldiner is to re-submit by email a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR#8090, email: casey.giselle@gmail.com.

MOTION For Ucl Preliminary Injunction To Stop Benjamin Moore Advertising
Off Calendar Jun-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MATTHEW WRENSHALL OF THE REED SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 1-213-457-8076 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
STAN SCHULDINER, TEL. NO. 1-711-761-8163, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STAN SCHULDINER'S MOTION For Preliminary Injunction is DENIED. To obtain a preliminary injunction for violation of the UCL per Business and Professions code 17078, Mr. Schuldiner must demonstrate a "clear violation of the Act." (E&H Wholesale, Inc. v. Glaser Bros. (1984) 158 Cal.App.3d 728, 733.) The court exercises its discretion whether to grant a preliminary injunction. (Id. (citing IT Corp. v. County of Imperial (1983) 35 Cal.3d 63, 69-70 ("[T]rial courts should evaluate two interrelated factors when deciding whether or not to issue a preliminary injunction. The first is the likelihood that the plaintiff will prevail on the merits at trial. The second is the interim harm that the plaintiff is likely to sustain if the injunction were denied as compared to the harm that the defendant is likely to suffer if the preliminary injunction were issued.").) Here, Mr. Schuldiner's declaration, which relies on hearsay, does not show a clear violation of the UCL. The order overruling the demurrer to Mr. Schuldiner's amended cross-complaint does not mandate granting this motion, which is an evidentiary motion, unlike a demurrer, which is a non-evidentiary motion. Mr. Schuldiner does not precisely define the scope of the proposed injunction, nor does he demonstrate imminent and irreparable harm, particularly since he first asserted his UCL claim more than a year ago. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Feb-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Stan Schuldiner, in pro per, Tel. 718.761.8163, for cross-complainant;
Matthew M. Wrenshall, of Reed Smith LLP, Tel. 213.457.8076, for cross-defendants ITC and Bancorp.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT REED SMITH LLP'S Anti-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE is GRANTED. The ninth count for breach of contract in the fourth amended cross-complaint arises from protected activity covered by the anti-SLAPP statute. The claim is based on petitioning activity as defined by CCP 425.16(e). At paragraph 40 of the fourth amended cross-complaint, Mr. Schuldiner seeks to recover for breach of the confidentiality agreement because Reed Smith "disclosed various embarrassing information in numerous court filings" and "filed the arbitration award." Reed Smith has thus met its first prong burden. Mr. Schuldiner has not satisfied his second prong burden of showing that there is minimal merit to his breach of contract claim because the litigation privilege bars that claim. Whether the litigation privilege applies to an action for breach of contract turns on whether its application furthers the policies underlying the privilege. (Vivian v. Labrucherie (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 267, 277; McNair v. City and County of San Francisco (2016) 5 Cal.App.5th 1154, 1169.) In his ninth claim Mr. Schuldiner is attempting to impose liability on Reed Smith for communicating with the court. "[T]he purpose of the litigation privilege is to ensure free access to the courts." (Wentland v. Wass (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1484, 1492.) Vivian applied the privilege in circumstances similar to this case because the parties settlement agreement did not "clearly prohibit" the challenged conduct. ITT Telecom Products Corp. v. Dooley (1989) 214 Cal.App.3d 307 held that the privilege did not apply to statements in breach of an express contract of confidentiality or nondisclosure. The defendant had signed an agreement not to disclose the plaintiff's trade secrets. The complaint alleged he breached this agreement by supplying information to a third party to assist in litigation. Balancing the society's interest in accurate judicial proceedings against the plaintiff's property interest in trade secrets and the defendant's written promise of nondisclosure, the court found the privilege did not apply. (Id. at p. 320.) The court noted there was no claim the disclosures were judicially compelled. (Id.) In this case, Reed Smith's disclosures were at least arguably necessary to confirm the arbitration award. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Feb-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stan Schuldiner, in pro per, Tel. 718.761.8163, for cross-complainant;
Matthew M. Wrenshall, of Reed Smith LLP, Tel. 213.457.8076, for cross-defendants ITC and Bancorp.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC. and THE BANCORP BANK'S DEMURRER to the fourth amended cross-complaint is OVERRULED as to the first, second, third, and seventh counts and off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on their motion to strike as to the fourth, fifth, sixth and eighth counts. Cross-defendants fail to show that the fourth amended cross-complaint is a sham pleading. Paragraph 20 of the second amended cross-complaint alleges "[r]espondent relied upon the representations that the cards were refundable and that they could be used at benjaminmoore.com and all Benjamin Moore physical stores, not merely some." This allegation is consistent with Mr. Schuldiner's present theory of reliance as alleged in the fourth amended cross-complaint. Cross-defendants do not cite clearly contradictory allegations. Paragraphs 11-12 and 16-18 of the fourth amended cross-complaint sufficiently allege damages. Paragraphs 5-7 of the fourth amended cross-complaint sufficiently allege a misrepresentation. The webpage, which cross-defendants improperly ask the court to take judicial notice of, does not disprove these allegations. Cross-defendants' res judicata argument lacks merit. Paragraph 19 of the second amended cross-complaint shows that the fraud claim arose in 2015 when Mr. Schuldiner attempted to make purchases at Benjamin Moore. That conduct occurred after the arbitration and nothing alleged in the fourth amended cross-complaint shows that res judicata applies. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Motion To Amend Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-24-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Matthew M. Wrenshall of the law offices of Reed Smith LLP (213.457.8076) appears via telephone as counsel for petitioner/cross-defendant/opposing party

Stan Schuldiner (PO Box 15203, San Francisco, CA 94115) appears In Propria Persona as respondent/cross-complainant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

RESPONDENT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT STAN SCHULDINER, Buyer's Motion To Amend Cross Complaint is DENIED. Mr. Schuldner does not need to "add Incomm as a defendant" because it is already a party to the case as reflected in the Notice of Name Change filed on November 30, 2016. (Schuldner's Motion to Amend 1:23.) Allowing amendment of paragraph 18 of the "Fourth Amended Cross-Claim" to plead more facts regarding Benjamin Moore would be inconsistent with this order entered on September 15, 2016. That order gave Mr. Schuldner his "last opportunity" to amend the claim. In addition, Mr. Schuldner does not show good cause for the amendment at this point in the litigation and his motion does not comply with CRC 3.1324(b). There is no basis to amend the "cross-claim" to plead a breach of contract claim. The motion to amend is based on alleged amendments to the cardholder agreement, but the record discloses that only one cardholder agreement is at issue and it has not been recently amended. The cardholder agreement that is attached to Mr. Schuldner's motion is the same agreement that is attached to petitioner's petition to confirm arbitration that was filed on June 30, 2015. The cancellation provision that Mr. Schuldner highlights as a basis for the proposed breach of contract claim was part of the arbitration and this litigation. Any amendment to the AAA rules that are part of the cardholder agreement are irrelevant to Mr. Schuldner's proposed claim. The proposed breach of contract cause of action is also contrary to Mr. Schuldner's position throughout this litigation that the parties did not enter into a contract. This court adopted that position when it denied petitioner's motion to compel arbitration and Mr. Schuldner is judicially estopped from now arguing the contrary. Petitioner's request for sanctions is denied because there was some arguable basis for the motion. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Respondent Stan Schuldner's motion for new trial is denied. Respondent brings this motion for new trial to vacate the arbitration award that the court confirmed on September 14, 2015. This untimely motion fails to highlight a basis for a new trial pursuant to CCP 657. The court has already confirmed the arbitration award and found that there was no basis to vacate the award under CCP 1286.2. On April 25, 2016, the court stated that: "Mr. Schuldner may no longer seek to vacate the arbitration award." Despite that order, respondent filed the instant motion. The court concludes that the motion is frivolous within the meaning of CCP 128.5 and Petitioner InComm Financial Services, Inc. fka ITC Financial Licenses, Inc. is entitled to $1,000 as reasonable sanctions to be paid by January 6, 2017. Buyer'S Motion For Leave To Use Pseudonym Or To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Matthew Wrenshall of Reed Smith LLP telephone number 213-457-8076 appeared by telephone for Petitioner ITC Financial Licenses, Inc.
Defendant Stan Schuldiner telephone number 718-761-8163 in Propria Persona.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STAN SCHULDINER'S Motion For Leave To Use Pseudonym Or To Amend Complaint is denied in part and granted in part as follows: Respondent Stan Schudiner's motion for leave to use pseudonym is DENIED. The use of a fictitious name to pursue, or defend against, claims impairs the public's right of access to court records, an important public right. (See Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 2.550(c).) In the case of pursuing claims, the use of pseudonyms also violates the statutory requirement that a complaint include "names of all the parties." (See Code Civ. Proc., 422.40.) Therefore, courts require a party seeking to use a pseudonym to demonstrate "exceptional circumstances." (Doe v. Lincoln Unified School Dist. (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 758, 767.) Schudiner has not demonstrated exceptional circumstances. Schindler's Fifth Amendment argument fails. Schudiner has not demonstrated a reasonable fear of incrimination. (See Troy v. Superior Court (1987) 186 Cal.App.3d 1006, 1012-1013.) Schudiner's motion to amend is GRANTED. Leave to amend must be granted liberally. (See Nestle v. City of Santa Monica (1972) 6 Cal.3d 920, 939.) The proposed amendment does not violate the court's September 14, 2016 order. Reasonably construed, the order provided that the third amended complaint would be Schudiner's last opportunity to plead the claims at issue in the demurrer; it was not a bar against all further amendments to the complaint regardless of subject matter. This ruling is without prejudice to any response or defense, including the litigation privilege. Schudiner is granted 10 days leave to amend. Order signed in open Court. The Court informs Mr. Schuldiner that the amendment the Court is authorizing is the amendment identified in the motion.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. (302/JQ)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Defendant Stan Schuldiner telephone number 718-761-8163 in Propria Persona.
Matthew Wrenshall of Reed Smith LLP telephone number 213-457-8076 appeared by telephone for Petitioners ITC Financial Licenses, Inc. and The Bancorp Bank.
Spencer Persson of Norton Rose Fulbright US LLP telephone number 213-892-9223 appeared by telephone for Cross-Defendant Office Depot Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling with the modification of allowing "60-days leave to amend instead of 20-days". CROSS-DEFENDANTS ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES and THE BANCORP BANK'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is: 1) sustained with sixty days leave to amend as to counts 1, 5-6,and 10 for violation of the Consumer Legal Remedies Act, violation of Business and Professions Code 17200, fraud and negligent misrepresentation and 2) sustained without leave to amend as to counts 2-4, 7-9, and 11-14 for breach of contract, account stated, unjust enrichment, conversion, Penal Code 496, breach of fiduciary duty, constructive trust, civil conspiracy, class treatment, and vacatur of the arbitration award. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted as to the arbitration award and denied as to webpage. The arbitration statutes and the confirmation of the arbitration award do not preclude Mr. Schuldner raising new claims that were not adjudicated by the arbitrator. Mr. Schuldner apparently acknowledges that his allegations of reliance are insufficient to allege claims for violation of the Consumer Legal Remedies Act, violation of Business and Professions Code 17200, fraud and negligent misrepresentation and is given leave to remedy that deficiency if he can do so in good faith. All of Mr. Schuldner's other claims fail as a matter of law and there does not appear any reasonable possibility that Mr. Schuldner will be able to state viable claims for breach of contract, account stated, unjust enrichment, conversion, Penal Code 496, breach of fiduciary duty, constructive trust, civil conspiracy, class treatment, and vacatur of the arbitration award. As a matter of law defendants did not breach a contract and no account stated, unjust enrichment, conversion, Penal Code 496 and constructive trust claim lies for sending gift cards in an amount $1 higher than Mr. Schuldner contends he should have received. The breach of fiduciary duty claim fails because defendants did not have a fiduciary relationship with Mr. Schuldner. Civil conspiracy is not a claim, but merely a way to hold one person or entity liable for the misconduct of another. As a pro per, Mr. Schuldner may not bring a class action. Mr. Schuldner may no longer seek to vacate the arbitration award. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation Pending Resolution Of Arbitration
Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MATTHEW WRENSHALL, ESQ. of Reed Smith LLP, Tel No. 213-457-8076 appearing telephonically for the Petitioner;
Respondent STAN SCHULDINER, Tel No. 718-701-8163 appearing in pro per.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PETITIONER ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation Pending Resolution Of Arbitration is denied. Petitioner fails to establish that the parties agreed to arbitrate. The contract indicates that acceptance of the offer to arbitrate is consummated by use of the card. The contract provides in pertinent part: "IF YOU DO NOT AGREE TO THE TERMS OF THIS ARBITRATION AGREEMENT, DO NOT ACTIVATE OR USE THE CASE'" The undisputed evidence shows that respondent never used the cards and therefore, there was no acceptance. Collateral estoppel does not apply because there was no prior action or final judgment on the merits. The court previously ruled that it was too late to overturn the arbitration award based on the argument that there was no agreement to arbitrate where Mr. Schuldiner requested arbitration and participated in the proceedings. Respondent's agreement to arbitrate the prior dispute does not mean that the instant dispute is subject to arbitration. The court notes that its register of actions does not reflect that the counter-claim was ever filed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Re Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Sep-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MATHEW WRENSHALL, ESQ. of Reed Smith LLP, Tel No. 213-457-8076 appearing telephonically for the Petitioner;
Respondent STAN SCHULDINER, Tel No. 718-761-8163 appearing in pro per.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PETITIONER ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC's Motion Re Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award, the motion is granted and the award is confirmed. Even if there were some service issues, Mr. Schuldiner opposes the petition to confirm on the merits. In addition, CCP 1290.4 does not require service of a summons and arbitration petitions can be heard on as little as 10-days notice under CCP 1290.2. The court continued the hearing for two weeks at Mr. Schuldiner's request. Mr. Schuldiner received more than enough time to respond. The award was binding and Mr. Schuldiner waived any argument that the arbitration clause did not cover the dispute. Mr. Schuldiner was the claimant who requested arbitration. ITC correctly cites Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 11, for the proposition that an error of law is not a basis to vacate an arbitration award. There is no basis to vacate the award/deny confirmation under CCP 1286.2. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: John K. Stewart; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/JKS)
